key_insight: A Monte Carlo framework converts published aggregate trial data into patient-level risk predictions for rare cancers, validated here in Ewing sarcoma.
clinical_relevance: It could improve survivorship surveillance, prognostic counseling, and future trial stratification when patient-level datasets are unavailable.
method: Six-stage discrete-event Monte Carlo simulation built from published trial aggregates.
Integrates genetic risk, serial biomarkers, ctDNA-MRD, competing mortality, and long-term toxicity modules.
Uses genotype-conditional weighting to improve multi-timepoint risk discrimination.
evidence_signal: In Ewing sarcoma, the framework matched published data with 3.2% mean absolute error across 23 efficacy endpoints and stayed within confidence intervals for all 20 toxicity endpoints.
watch_item: Results are validated in one rare cancer from published aggregate data, so broader transfer to other cancers still needs prospective confirmation.
paper_title: Multi-Timepoint Risk Stratification in Rare Cancers: A Computational Framework Validated against Published Ewing Sarcoma Trial Data
